key_insight: The paper builds lower-regularity finite element elasticity complexes on tetrahedral Alfeld splits that reduce Sobolev smoothness and polynomial degree while preserving exactness and hybridizable symmetric-stress conformity.
engineering_relevance: This enables more practical 3D elasticity discretizations with fewer continuity requirements, no vertex stress degrees of freedom, and a path to efficient high-order hybridizable stress formulations.
method: Construct local polynomial elasticity complexes from Bernstein-Gelfand-Gelfand arguments on Alfeld-split tetrahedra.
Derive two exact sequences: an $H^2$-$H^1(\mathrm{inc})$ complex and a lower-regularity $H^1(\mathrm{curl})$-$H(\mathrm{inc}^+)$ complex.
Use bubble subcomplexes, dimension formulas, and commuting interpolation to assemble global finite element subcomplexes.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports two proved local exact sequences, unisolvent finite elements, global finite element subcomplexes, and commuting interpolation diagrams, but no numerical experiments.
watch_item: Implementation may be delicate because the spaces rely on Alfeld-split tetrahedral constructions and specialized commuting/interpolation machinery.
paper_title: Low-regularity finite element elasticity complexes with hybridizable stresses on tetrahedral Alfeld splits
